verses.text: After six years Jehoiada the priest takes courage, binds the captains by covenant, and shows them the hidden king's son. Faith at last moves boldly. There is a time to wait in secret and a time to act, and the godly know when the hour has come.

After six years Jehoiada the priest takes courage, binds the captains by covenant, and shows them the hidden king's son. Faith at last moves boldly. There is a time to wait in secret and a time to act, and the godly know when the hour has come.

After six years Jehoiada the priest takes courage, binds the captains by covenant, and shows them the hidden king's son. Faith at last moves boldly. There is a time to wait in secret and a time to act, and the godly know when the hour has come.

Jehoiada orders the guards' stations around the temple and the young king, charging that none unauthorized come near. He guards the work with careful order. Zeal for God is wise as well as bold, planning each step to protect what is sacred.

They bring out the boy, set the crown on him, give him the testimony, anoint him, and shout, 'God save the king.' David's heir is openly enthroned. God's promise, long hidden, breaks into the light, and the rightful king is owned at last.

Athaliah hears the noise, rushes in crying 'Treason!', and Jehoiada has her led out and slain. The usurper meets her end. The throne stolen by blood is repaid in kind, and God's justice removes the tyrant in His time.

Jehoiada makes a covenant that they should be the LORD's people, and they tear down Baal's temple and kill his priest. Renewal of the king is joined to renewal of worship. A right ruler is not enough; the people must also return to their God.

Jehoiada makes a covenant that they should be the LORD's people, and they tear down Baal's temple and kill his priest. Renewal of the king is joined to renewal of worship. A right ruler is not enough; the people must also return to their God.

Jehoiada restores the temple offices, brings the king to his throne, and the whole city rejoices and is quiet. Worship, order, and gladness return together. When God's house and God's king are set right, peace flows out to the people.
